_comment: Creator OS source registry — citation graph. Written and updated exclusively by tools/source_currency.py and tools/traversal_engine.py. Do not edit by hand. Intervals governed by canonical-sources/traversal-config.json.

sources.name: Google Search Central Blog
sources.extraction_hint: Look for algorithm update announcements, ranking signal changes, structured data spec updates, and Core Web Vitals threshold changes.

sources.name: YouTube Creator Blog
sources.extraction_hint: Look for algorithm changes affecting discovery, Shorts monetization updates, chapter and timestamps spec changes, and creator program policy updates.

sources.name: Google Search Status Dashboard
sources.extraction_hint: Check for active or recent incidents that could explain ranking flux. Flag to seo-keywords when an incident is active.

sources.name: Search Engine Journal
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for algorithm update coverage, YouTube SEO guides, and home decor content strategy articles. Flag T3 confidence on any data cited from here.

sources.name: Search Engine Roundtable
sources.extraction_hint: Early signals on Google algorithm changes (often surfaces before official confirmation). Useful for trend-check when SEJ has nothing.

sources.name: YouTube Help: Video formats and specs
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to: accepted file formats, resolution limits, aspect ratio specs, upload size limits, Shorts length limits.

sources.name: YouTube Help: Titles, descriptions, and tags
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to title char limits, description char limits, tag count and length limits, chapter timestamp rules.

sources.name: Instagram Help: Reels formats and captions
sources.extraction_hint: Check for caption character limit changes, Reel length limit changes, cover image specs, hashtag strategy updates.

sources.name: TikTok Creator Portal
sources.extraction_hint: Check for video length changes, caption char limit updates, algorithm guidance for home decor niche, monetization threshold changes.

sources.name: Pinterest Business: Pin formats and specs
sources.extraction_hint: Check for title char limit changes, description char limit changes, idea pin format updates, alt text requirements.

sources.name: YouTube Data API v3 Revision History
sources.extraction_hint: Look for deprecated fields or endpoints (especially captions.list, videos.list, search.list), quota changes, and new scopes or parameters.

sources.name: Meta Graph API Changelog
sources.extraction_hint: Check for deprecated fields (e.g. impressions deprecated April 2025, replaced by views), new Instagram Login scope changes, Graph API version deprecations.

sources.name: TikTok for Developers API Changelog
sources.extraction_hint: Check for Display API endpoint changes, new fields in /v2/video/list/, access scope changes, quota limit updates.

sources.name: Microsoft Graph API Changelog
sources.extraction_hint: Check for /me/drive/ endpoint changes, OneDrive file access permission scope updates, deprecated fields or versions.

sources.name: Anthropic Release Notes
sources.extraction_hint: Check for new model releases, MCP spec updates, tool use changes, context window changes, and deprecated API versions.

sources.name: Influencer Marketing Hub Benchmark Report
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: CPM rates by subscriber tier, rate ranges for sponsored YouTube videos and integrations, home decor niche premium factors. Note the report year.

sources.name: Later Creator Economy Report
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: Instagram Reel sponsored rate ranges by follower tier, engagement rate benchmarks by niche, brand deal structure trends.

sources.name: Linqia State of Influencer Marketing
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: content bundle deal structure trends, Pinterest rate benchmarks, negotiation norms, usage rights pricing.

sources.name: CreatorIQ Annual Benchmark Report
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: engagement rate benchmarks by platform and niche, performance benchmarks for home decor category, brand spend trends.

sources.name: Claude Code Release Notes
sources.extraction_hint: Check for new slash commands, MCP server support changes, permission model updates, hook system changes.

sources.name: MCP Specification (modelcontextprotocol.io)
sources.extraction_hint: Check for protocol version changes, new transport mechanisms, resource/tool/prompt type updates that affect skill plugin compatibility.

sources.name: Google Drive MCP Connector Docs
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to available tool names, authentication flow changes, file access permission scope updates.

sources.name: YouTube Help: How recommendations work
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: current recommendation signals, what YouTube says it rewards, what it says creators should focus on. This is the official source for algorithm signal claims.

sources.name: YouTube Help: Understand and improve channel analytics
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: metric definitions (CTR, AVD, impressions, watch time), how to read the traffic sources report, click-through benchmarks, audience retention curve interpretation.

sources.name: YouTube Creator Academy: Grow your channel
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for new lessons on thumbnail strategy, title optimization, end screen and card best practices, Shorts growth guidance. Traversal depth 2 to capture linked studies.

sources.name: Google Search Central: SEO Starter Guide
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: title tag best practices, meta description guidance, structured data recommendations, mobile-first indexing requirements. Traversal finds linked technical documentation.

sources.name: Google Search Central: Video best practices
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: VideoObject schema requirements, video sitemap specs, thumbnail requirements for video rich results, key moments / chapter markup.

sources.name: Google Structured Data: VideoObject schema
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: required and recommended VideoObject properties, Clip and SeekToAction markup for chapter links, testing tool URL. Changes here affect how videos appear in Google Search.

sources.name: Google Search Central: How Google evaluates content quality
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: E-E-A-T signals (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness), helpful content criteria, what qualifies as low-quality or thin content. Crucial for content strategy quality gates.

sources.name: Pinterest Creator Hub: SEO and discovery
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: current Pinterest search trends and predictions, keyword guidance for home decor category, algorithm changes to pin distribution. Annual Predicts report is high priority.

sources.name: Pinterest Newsroom
sources.extraction_hint: Check for algorithm changes, new ad format announcements, creator program updates. Early signal for platform spec changes before help center updates.

sources.name: TikTok Creator Portal: Understanding the For You Page
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: what TikTok says drives FYP distribution (completion rate, interactions, video info, device/account settings), content quality guidance, hashtag role.

sources.name: TikTok Newsroom
sources.extraction_hint: Check for algorithm transparency updates, new creator monetization features, policy changes. Early signal before creator portal updates.

sources.name: FTC: Disclosures 101 for Social Media Influencers
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: required disclosure language, placement requirements (beginning of description, verbal in video), what counts as a material connection. Changes here affect safety.md and deal contract templates.

sources.name: FTC: Endorsement Guides
sources.extraction_hint: Check for updates to the formal endorsement guides (16 CFR Part 255). Pay attention to social media platform-specific guidance and AI-generated content rules.

sources.name: Moz Blog: YouTube SEO and video search
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for video SEO tactical guides, CTR optimization studies, keyword research methodology articles. Flag T3 confidence. Traversal finds cited studies and data sources.

sources.name: Ahrefs Blog: Video SEO
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: video ranking factor studies, keyword research for YouTube, competitor video analysis methodology. Flag T3. Traversal finds linked research.

sources.name: vidIQ Creator Academy
sources.extraction_hint: YouTube-specific SEO tactics, keyword research methodology, Shorts optimization. Lower confidence than T1/T2 but useful for niche-specific YouTube tactics. Traversal finds trending keyword examples.

sources.name: The Spruce: Home Decor
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for trending home decor topics, new style names (e.g. '[trending style name]', '[trending style name]'), DIY technique coverage. Extract: what is being written about, what angles are saturated, what is underserved. High editorial authority in the niche.

sources.name: Apartment Therapy
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for renter-friendly DIY coverage, small-space organization, vintage and thrifting trend articles. Strong overlap with The Renter and Vintage Hunter personas. High editorial authority.

sources.name: HGTV: Decorating ideas
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: which room types and project types receive the most editorial coverage, which aesthetic terms appear in headlines, seasonal and trend features. Use for competitor-scan context.

sources.name: Better Homes and Gardens: Decorating
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: trending style names, DIY and makeover approaches being covered, seasonal content patterns. BHG is a mainstream authority whose coverage signals broad market awareness of a trend.

sources.name: Sprout Social: Social media statistics and benchmarks
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: engagement rate benchmarks by platform and industry (home/lifestyle niche), best posting times, reach benchmarks. Note report date and sample size if stated.

sources.name: Buffer: State of Social Media Report
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: platform-level engagement benchmarks, creator posting frequency data, video vs. image performance comparisons. Annual report — note year.

sources.name: TikTok Content Posting API
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to upload endpoint URLs, rate limit values, new required fields (especially is_aigc), scope name changes, and chunked upload protocol updates.

sources.name: TikTok Research API
sources.extraction_hint: Check for new fields in video/comment queries (favorites_count added May 2026, display_name added May 2026), quota changes, and access tier requirement changes.

sources.name: TikTok Display API
sources.extraction_hint: Check for endpoint URL changes, rate limit updates, and new fields in /v2/video/list/ or /v2/user/info/.

sources.name: TikTok Login Kit
sources.extraction_hint: Check for OAuth flow changes, token lifetime changes (access token 24h, refresh 365d as of 2026), and new platform support.

sources.name: TikTok Newsroom: How TikTok recommends videos
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: official FYP ranking signals (rewatch rate now #1, surpassing completion), what TikTok says are non-factors (follower count, prior video performance), signals that make content FYP-ineligible. This is the primary official source for algorithm transparency.

sources.name: Meta Graph API: Instagram content publishing
sources.extraction_hint: Check for: new media_type values, new post_info fields (trial_params added December 2025), metric deprecations (impressions deprecated April 2025, replaced by views), scope name changes (instagram_business_basic is current). Note API version.

sources.name: Instagram: How Reels are ranked and recommended
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: official ranking signals for Reels (sends per reach as unique primary signal, watch time, saves, originality gate at 10+ reposts), what Instagram says it penalizes (low-res, watermarked content, reposts), hashtag guidance updates.

sources.name: Pinterest API v5 Documentation
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to: video pin upload flow (register → S3 upload → create pin), rate limit values (reads 1000/min, writes 100/min, analytics 200/min), scope name changes, and new analytics metrics.

sources.name: YouTube Help: YouTube Shorts
sources.extraction_hint: Check for changes to: maximum Shorts length (3 minutes as of Oct 2024), YPP Shorts eligibility thresholds (1,000 subs + 10M views in 90 days), revenue share percentages, and any changes to how Shorts are identified.

sources.name: YouTube Creator Blog: How Shorts are distributed
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: current primary Shorts ranking signal (session contribution is now primary as of 2025, replacing swipe-through-rate), AVD thresholds for distribution, three-phase distribution model, freshness decay window (~28-30 days). Signal stack changes over time.

sources.name: Google Trends
sources.extraction_hint: Use gprop='youtube' parameter for YouTube-specific trend data. Check home decor and DIY seasonal patterns. Breakout keywords (>5000% growth) signal emerging demand before it appears in autocomplete. All volume data labeled [estimated, unverified].

sources.name: Sprout Social: Social media industry benchmarks
sources.extraction_hint: Extract: engagement rate benchmarks by platform and industry vertical (home/lifestyle), optimal posting frequency data, reach benchmarks for micro-creators. Note report date and methodology.

sources.name: vidIQ: YouTube Shorts optimization guide
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for CTR benchmark data (vidIQ analyzed 5.08M channels: <3% poor, 3-5% good, 7%+ excellent), Shorts algorithm updates, hook format recommendations with retention data. Flag T3 confidence.

sources.name: TubeBuddy: YouTube growth tips
sources.extraction_hint: Scan for: SEO optimization tactics, chapter marker best practices, end screen strategy, keyword research approaches for YouTube. Flag T3 confidence.

sources.name: Pinterest API v5 create-pin
sources.extraction_hint: pins-create endpoint: media registration, board_id, required scopes (pins:write); watch for v6 or field changes

sources.name: YouTube Data API v3 videos.insert
sources.extraction_hint: resumable upload endpoint, quota cost, required scopes; watch for quota changes

sources.name: Instagram Content Publishing API
sources.extraction_hint: two-step container then media_publish flow; rate limits; trial_params; watch for Graph API version sunsets

sources.name: FTC Endorsement Guides: What People Are Asking (FAQ)
sources.extraction_hint: practical dos and don'ts on disclosure placement, wording, and platform tools; answers common creator questions

sources.name: FTC Endorsements, Influencers, and Reviews hub
sources.extraction_hint: landing page linking current FTC endorsement guidance and enforcement updates for influencers

sources.name: U.S. Copyright Office: What Is Copyright
sources.extraction_hint: copyright ownership basics; who holds rights in created content; useful for usage-rights and ownership clause review

sources.name: Cornell LII Wex: Contract
sources.extraction_hint: plain-language definition of contract terms and formation; general reference only, not case law

sources.name: Cornell LII Wex: License
sources.extraction_hint: plain-language definition of a license and its scope; supports usage-rights and licensing clause review

sources.name: FTC Soliciting and Paying for Online Reviews: A Guide for Marketers
sources.extraction_hint: rules on paid and incentivized reviews and endorsements; relevant to gifted and affiliate deal terms

sources.name: The Home Depot product pricing (DIY materials and tools)
sources.extraction_hint: current retail prices for project materials (lumber, paint, hardware, fasteners) and tools; per-item vendor pricing pages, not aggregators

sources.name: Lowe's product pricing (DIY materials and tools)
sources.extraction_hint: current retail prices for project materials and tools; per-item vendor pricing pages for cross-vendor comparison against Home Depot
